data.title: Redwood Trust's Sequoia platform gets $8B loan boost via Castlelake JV
data.description:
• Redwood Trust (RWT), a mortgage REIT, and Castlelake, a global credit investor, announced on Wednesday a joint venture to grow Redwood’s Sequoia platform and expand prime jumbo mortgage loan buying.
  • The JV plans to purchase up to $8B of high-quality home loans.
  • Under the setup, Sequoia will source, review, and package eligible mortgage loans, focusing on high-quality assets to ensure smooth and disciplined execution.
  • Redwood Trust (RWT) via Sequoia bought ~$100B in loans and turned over $50B+ into securities, while Castlelake has invested over ~$10B in real estate loans since 2024.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Intellia Therapeutics prices $180M stock offering at discount
data.description:
• Intellia Therapeutics (NTLA) on Wednesday said it priced an underwritten public offering of 16.74M shares at $10.75 each, for expected gross proceeds of about $180M. 
  • Shares down -11% in premarket. The stock closed at $13.2 on Tuesday.
  • The company is selling all shares in the offering. It also granted underwriters a 30-day option to purchase up to an additional 2.51M shares at the same price, less discounts and commissions.
  • Jefferies, Goldman Sachs, and Citigroup are acting as joint book-running managers.

data.title: John Marshall Bancorp GAAP EPS of $0.43 beats by $0.03, revenue of $16.79M beats by $0.31M
data.description:
• John Marshall Bancorp press release (JMSB): Q1 GAAP EPS of $0.43 beats by $0.03.
  • Revenue of $16.79M (+15.0% Y/Y) beats by $0.31M.
  • Growing Book Value per Share and Dividends – Book value per share increased from $17.72 as of March 31, 2025 to $19.00 as of March 31, 2026, a 7.2% increase. On April 28, 2026, the Company’s Board of Directors declared a quarterly cash dividend of $0.09 per share on the Company’s common stock.

data.title: Google expands Gemini's 'Memories' feature to UK
data.description:
Google (GOOG)(GOOGL) is expanding Gemini's "Memories," a feature that uses past chats to make better recommendations and lead to "more natural and relevant conversations," to the U.K. market, the company announced today.

They also added a tool that allows users to copy and paste full chat histories with other chatbots, such as OpenAI's (OPENAI) ChatGPT or Anthropic's (ANTHRO) Claude, directly into Gemini. This helps users build up their conversational background with the AI assistant.

"We believe that the most helpful AI assistant is one that's personal to you, and understands your preferences and past conversations," said Maryam Sanglaji, group product manager of the Gemini app, in a blog post. "But if you're curious to try a different option, starting over with an assistant that doesn't know you can feel daunting."

The memory feature was first released for subscribers of the Google One AI Premium plan in November 2024. It has since been updated and enhanced. However, it was not made available in the European Economic Area, Switzerland, or the U.K. They will be rolling it out in the U.K. over the next couple of weeks.

data.title: GH Research prices $117.5M share offering
data.description:
• GH Research said on Wednesday it priced an underwritten U.S. offering of 6.53M ordinary shares at $18 each, for gross proceeds of about $117.5M
  • The company is selling all of the shares in the offering, which is expected to close on April 30.
  • The transaction was initiated by Lynx1 Capital Management, with participation from Deep Track Capital and Foresite Capital.
  • Stifel, Cantor Fitzgerald and RBC Capital Markets are acting as joint book-running managers. Canaccord Genuity and Citizens Capital Markets are co-lead managers.
  • Shares down -1% premarket.

data.title: Wholesale, retail inventories accelerate M/M in March
data.description:
Wholesale inventories climbed 1.4% M/M to $932.8B in March, compared with the +0.4% consensus and +0.9% prior (revised from +0.8%), according to preliminary data released by the U.S. Census Bureau on Wednesday. Y/Y, wholesale inventories increased 2.9%.

Retail inventories for March were estimated at an end-of-month level of $823.5B, up 0.7% sequentially (vs. +0.3% prior) and +2.3% from a year ago. 

Excluding autos, retail inventories were up 0.5% M/M in March, vs. the revised +0.4% prior.

data.title: AbbVie hikes 2026 outlook as Skyrizi, Rinvoq offset Humira slump
data.description:
AbbVie (ABBV) shares added ~2% in the premarket on Thursday after the company raised its full-year earnings outlook as its newer immunology drugs, Skyrizi and Rinvoq, offset weakness in its former best-seller, Humira, driving a Q1 2026 topline beat for the Illinois-based pharma giant.

The company added $15B in revenue for the quarter with ~12% YoY growth, exceeding the consensus by $280M, as Skyrizi and Rinvoq added $4.5B and $2.1B globally with ~31% YoY and ~23% YoY growth, respectively.

Meanwhile, Humira, once the world’s best-selling medication, generated $688M with a ~39% YoY decline as its off-patent versions hurt its sales.

AbbVie’s (ABBV) aesthetics portfolio brought in $1.6B with ~ ~8% YoY growth driven by its anti-wrinkle therapy Botox, which generated $668M with ~20% YoY growth. Meanwhile, the neuroscience portfolio added $2.9B with ~26% YoY growth as the therapeutic version of Botox recorded $1.0B in revenue with ~17% YoY growth.

During the quarter, ABBV’s adjusted gross and operating margins reached 83.6% and 40.8%, down from 84.1% and 42.3% in the prior year period, while its adjusted diluted EPS stood at $2.65 with ~8% YoY growth but was missing the consensus by $0.02.

The company estimated $14.08 - $14.28 of adjusted EPS for 2026, in line with $14.21 projected by analysts and up from its prior forecast of $13.96 - $14.16.

data.title: Earnings Snapshot: Teva Pharmaceutical Industries tops Q1 estimates; maintains 2026 business outlook
Awareness Group appoints Cole Farmer as COO
data.description:
• The Awareness Group (TAAG) on Wednesday said it appointed Cole Farmer as chief operating officer as the company expands its renewable energy platform.
  • Farmer previously founded Core Energy Group, where he helped build operations across residential and commercial solar markets before its sale to SunPower (SPWR).
  • In his new role, Farmer will oversee national operations, deployment strategy and supply chain initiatives.

data.title: Durable goods rebound more than expected in March
data.description:
March new orders for manufactured durable goods: +0.8% M/M vs. +0.5% consensus and -1.2% prior (revised from -1.4%), according to data released by the U.S. Census Bureau on Wednesday.

New orders, excluding transportation: +0.9% M/M vs. +0.4% consensus and +1.2% prior (revised from +0.8%).

New orders, excluding defense: -0.3% M/M vs. -1.2% prior.

Developing… Check back for updates.
